__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics (the same source the #518 raw-metric dashboards use).

__inputs.name: DS_INSIGHTS
__inputs.label: Insights API (JSON / Infinity)
__inputs.description: JSON/Infinity datasource pointed at the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API: GET /api/v1/observability/insights and /health. See dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md for the datasource setup. Plugin: yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource (or grafana-json-datasource).

list.name: Insight anomalies
list.$comment: ADR 047 anomaly markers. Each anomalies[] item supplies detected_at -> annotation time, explanation -> hover text, severity -> tag (drives marker color via Grafana annotation styling), metric -> title. Sourced from GET /api/v1/observability/insights (see README 'How anomaly annotations are sourced'). Until ADR 047 ships, this query returns nothing and the charts render with deploy annotations only.

list.name: CalVer deploys
list.$comment: CalVer (YYYY.M.D.N) deploy markers. deploys[] is part of the insights digest payload; deployed_at -> time, version (e.g. 2026.5.28.1) -> hover text. Lets you correlate an anomaly with the release that introduced it.

description: mdk - daily insights (insight-fed). The intelligence layer on top of the #518 golden-signal dashboards: a narrative banner, per-project health gauges, a 'Top 3 to look at today' watchlist, and the golden-signal charts overlaid with anomaly + deploy annotations. The top rows read from the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (GET /api/v1/observability/insights) via a JSON/Infinity datasource; the time-series charts read the same raw OTel metrics as #518 (mdk.job.duration_ms, mdk.jobs.completed, mdk.run.cost_usd) from Prometheus. See dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md. NOTE: ADR 047 endpoints are not on main yet - until they ship, the insight panels render their documented-gap state (banner explains this) and the metric charts work standalone.

panels.title: What happened & what to look at (start here)

options.content:
## mdk - daily insights

**What this shows.** The *intelligence* layer over the #518 golden-signal dashboards. Instead of raw charts you read first, the top rows answer **"what happened yesterday and what should I look at today?"** from the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence layer (`observability_insights` store + `GET /api/v1/observability/insights`): a narrative digest, per-project **health scores**, a **Top 3** watchlist, and anomaly markers overlaid on the golden-signal charts.

**Normal.** All health gauges green (>= 80); the Top 3 table short or empty; the narrative reads "nominal"; no red anomaly markers on the charts.

**If you see X.** Start at the **Top 3 to look at today** panel; each row links straight to the Jaeger trace + the matching run. Then scan the gauges for the red/amber project, then drop into the anomaly-annotated charts below (hover a red marker for the explanation).

> **Gap banner (ADR 047 not on main yet).** The narrative banner, health gauges, Top-3 table, and anomaly/deploy annotations read from `GET /api/v1/observability/insights`, which is delivered by **ADR 047** (Observability Intelligence). That endpoint is **not on `main` at the time this pack shipped.** Until it is, those panels show empty/placeholder data (Grafana will surface a datasource error on the insight panels) - this is expected, not a misconfiguration. The Prometheus metric charts below work standalone today. Wire the JSON/Infinity datasource per `dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md` once ADR 047 is deployed.

**Cross-surface.** Raw metrics: `dashboards/grafana/mdk-golden-signals.json` (#518). Azure equivalent: `infra/azure-monitor/workbooks/insights.workbook.json`.

panels.title: Narrative digest (yesterday / watch)
panels.description: Renders narrative_digest (markdown) from GET /api/v1/observability/insights. The digest is a short 'Yesterday: ... Watch: ...' summary the ADR 047 layer composes from the day's anomalies, top failures, and trends. Until ADR 047 ships this panel is empty (see gap banner above).

options.content:
_Awaiting `narrative_digest` from `GET /api/v1/observability/insights` (ADR 047)._

When the insights API is wired, this panel renders the latest narrative markdown, e.g.:

> **Yesterday.** 12,400 runs across 4 projects; success 99.2%. `acme-support` p95 latency rose 38% after deploy `2026.5.28.1` - traced to a slower rerank stage. Cost flat.
>
> **Watch.** `acme-support` latency anomaly (amber); `internal-rag` token volume trending up 3 days running.

To render live markdown from the API, replace this static text panel with a **Business Text** (or **Dynamic Text**) panel bound to the `${DS_INSIGHTS}` datasource, `root_selector: narrative_digest`. The core Grafana `text` panel cannot template a datasource field, so the live wiring uses that community panel - documented in the README.

panels.title: Fleet health (worst project)
panels.description: Lowest health_score across projects in scope, from GET /api/v1/observability/insights. Thresholds: green >= 80, amber 50-79, red < 50.

panels.title: Health gauge per project
panels.description: One gauge per project from health[].health_score (GET /api/v1/observability/insights). Color thresholds: red < 50, amber 50-79, green >= 80 (documented in the README). The score is the ADR 047 composite of success rate, latency-vs-baseline, cost-vs-baseline, and eval/quality signals.

options.content:
**What.** Composite **health_score** (0-100) per project from `GET /api/v1/observability/health` (ADR 047).

**Color map.**

- **Green** `>= 80` - nominal.
- **Amber** `50-79` - degraded; check the narrative + Top 3.
- **Red** `< 50` - one or more SLOs breached or a hard anomaly.

**If amber/red.** Open **Top 3 to look at** below for that project, then the anomaly-annotated charts. The score blends success rate, latency-vs-baseline, cost-vs-baseline, and eval pass-rate (ADR 047 scoring).

panels.title: Top 3 to look at today (anomalies + top failures, by severity)
Top 3 to look at today
panels.description: Merged anomalies[] + top_failures[] from GET /api/v1/observability/insights, sorted by severity (critical > high > medium > low), top 3. Each row links to the Jaeger trace search and the matching run. Until ADR 047 ships this table is empty (see gap banner).

panels.title: Sub-panel: how to use the Top 3

options.content:
**What.** The day's most-important things, merged from the insights `anomalies[]` (statistical deviations) and `top_failures[]` (clustered errors), sorted by `severity` (critical > high > medium > low), capped at 3.

**Normal.** Empty or a single low-severity row.

**If a row is present.** Click **trace** to open the representative Jaeger trace, **run** to open the matching run in the project-health dashboard. Triage like #518: latency anomaly -> p95-by-kind + Jaeger; error cluster -> failed runs in Langfuse + provider 5xx; cost anomaly -> token volume + eval drift.

**Color map.** `critical` dark-red, `high` red, `medium` orange, `low` yellow (matches the README severity table).

panels.title: Golden signals with anomaly + deploy annotations (raw metrics, #518 catalog)

panels.description: Job latency p95 (mdk.job.duration_ms) overlaid with the 'Insight anomalies' and 'CalVer deploys' annotations defined on this dashboard. Red markers = anomalies[] from the insights API (hover for the explanation); blue markers = deploys. Same metric + 30s SLO line as the #518 golden-signals dashboard.
panels.title: Latency p95/p99 with anomaly markers (red line = high-latency-p95 SLO @ 30s)

panels.description: Error + dead-letter rate (mdk.jobs.completed) overlaid with the insight anomaly + deploy annotations. Red markers correlate an error spike with its explanation; blue markers with the deploy that introduced it. Same metric + 10% SLO line as #518.
panels.title: Error & dead-letter rate with anomaly markers (red line = high-error-rate SLO @ 10%)

panels.description: LLM spend rate (USD/hr) per tenant from mdk.run.cost_usd, overlaid with the insight anomaly + deploy annotations. A red marker on a cost step-change points to the anomaly explanation (e.g. token regression after a deploy). Same metric as the #518 cost dashboard.
panels.title: Cost rate (USD/hr) by tenant with anomaly markers
Sub-panel: how the annotations are sourced

options.content:
**What.** The charts above are the same raw OTel metrics as #518 (`mdk.job.duration_ms`, `mdk.jobs.completed`, `mdk.run.cost_usd`), but with two **dashboard annotation queries** layered on top:

- **Insight anomalies** (red) - from `anomalies[]` on `GET /api/v1/observability/insights`. Each item's `detected_at` places the marker; `explanation` is the hover text; `severity` drives the color. Defined under this dashboard's `annotations.list` (datasource `${DS_INSIGHTS}`).
- **CalVer deploys** (blue) - from `deploys[]` on the same endpoint (`deployed_at` + `version` like `2026.5.28.1`). Lets you line up an anomaly with the release that caused it.

**Normal.** No red markers; sparse blue markers at deploy times.

**If you see X.** Hover a red marker for the explanation, then drill via the panel's Jaeger link or the **Top 3** table above. **Gap:** until ADR 047 ships, the annotation queries return nothing and only the metric lines render.

title: mdk - daily insights (narrative, health, top 3, anomalies)
